seed_instruction: Create a financial plan for a student working part-time to pay for college tuition and living expenses.
org_constraint: The response must be formatted in JSON with the following keys: "Income", "Expenses", "Savings", and "Strategies".
new_constraint: The response must be formatted in Markdown with at least two level-2 headers.

## Student Financial Plan Overview

This document outlines a comprehensive financial strategy for a full-time university student balancing academic responsibilities with part-time employment. The plan aims to cover tuition fees, housing, food, and miscellaneous expenses while building an emergency savings buffer.

```json
{
  "Income": [
    {
      "Source": "Part-time Employment",
      "Estimate_Per_Hour": "$14.00",
      "Hours_Per_Week": 20,
      "Monthly_Estimation": "$1,120.00"
    },
    {
      "Source": "Federal Pell Grant",
      "Amount": "$3,500.00"
    },
    {
      "Source": "University Work-Study",
      "Estimated_Annual": "$4,000.00"
    }
  ],
  "Expenses": {
    "Tuition_Fees": "$9,500.00",
    "Housing_Rent": "$800.00",
    "Food_Groceries": "$400.00",
    "Transportation": "$150.00",
    "Books_Supplies": "$200.00",
    "Personal_Care_Misc": "$200.00"
  },
  "Savings": {
    "Emergency_Fund_Target": "$1,000.00",
    "Academic_Future_Fund": "High_Yield_Savings_Account",
    "Contribution_Rate": "10_percent_of_net_income"
  },
  "Strategies": [
    "Track all transactions weekly using a budget app.",
    "Cook meals in bulk to reduce grocery costs by 20%.",
    "Utilize library resources instead of purchasing textbooks.",
    "Apply for all available merit-based scholarships annually.",
    "Review bank statements monthly for recurring subscriptions.",
    "Negotiate utility bills and set up autopay to avoid overdrafts."
  ]
}
```

## Monthly Cash Flow Management

To maintain financial stability throughout the semester, the student must adhere to a strict cash flow management schedule. Income received from wages should not be mixed directly with living funds; rather, a tiered allocation system is recommended. Approximately 60% of net income goes toward immediate living expenses (Rent/Food), 30% is directed toward debt service or loan payments, and 10% is reserved strictly for savings goals. This structure prevents overspending during weeks when hourly wage hours are reduced due to class schedules. Additionally, tracking expenses against the plan provided above in the previous section ensures long-term solvency and reduces reliance on high-interest credit cards.<|im_end|>
